original_abstract: The effectiveness of machine learning models can be significantly averse to redundant and irrelevant features present in the large dataset which can cause drastic perfor‑ mance degradation. This paper proposes IGRF-RFE: a hybrid feature selection method tasked for multi-class network anomalies using a multilayer perceptron (MLP) network. IGRF-RFE exploits the qualities of both a filter method for its speed and a wrapper
humanized_abstract: Machine learning models can degrade significantly by redundant and irrelevant features of large datasets. In this paper, a hybrid feature selection method known as IGRF-RFE has been proposed for multi-class network anomalies classification. A multilayer perceptron (MLP) network is used as a classifier. The IGRF-RFE exploits the advantages of both filter and wrapper methods. The filter method is faster than the wrapper method. On the other hand, the wrapper method always provides better accuracy than the filter method. The filter method - IGRF is employed for quick feature reduction. The reduced features are then evaluated by the wrapper method - RFE. Experimental results justify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

The reduced features are then evaluated by the wrapper method - RFE. Experimental results justify the effectiveness of the proposed method.
